emit: verb. To send out or give off.
imine: noun. Any of a class of organic nitrogen compounds having the general formula R 2 C=NR; they are tautomeric with enamines.
imminent: adjective. About to happen, occur, or take place very soon, especially of something which won't last long.
inn: symbol. ISO 639-3 language code for Isinai.
inti: noun. The currency of Peru between 1985 and 1991, replacing the sol.
intine: noun. The innermost wall of a spore or pollen grain
mem: symbol. ISO 639-3 language code for Mangala.
men: symbol. ISO 639-2 & ISO 639-3 language code for Mende (Sierra Leone).
met: symbol. ISO 639-3 language code for Mato.
mien: noun. Demeanor; facial expression or attitude, especially one which is intended by its bearer.
mim: symbol. ISO 639-3 language code for Alacatlatzala Mixtec.
mime: noun. A form of acting without words; pantomime.
mine: pronoun. That or those belonging to me.
minim: noun. A half note, drawn as a semibreve with a stem.
mint: noun. A building or institution where money (originally, only coins) is produced under government licence.
mite: noun. Any of many minute arachnids which, along with the ticks, comprise subclass Acari (syn.
net: symbol. ISO 639-3 language code for Nete.
nim: symbol. ISO 639-3 language code for Nilamba.
nine: noun. NATO & ICAO radiotelephony clear code (spelling-alphabet name) for the digit 9.
nit: symbol. ISO 639-3 language code for Southeastern Kolami.
ten: noun. NATO, ICAO, ITU & IMO radiotelephony code for 10, used only with o'clock to indicate direction.
time: noun. The inevitable progression into the future with the passing of present and past events.
tin: symbol. ISO 639-3 language code for Tindi.
tine: noun. A spike or point on an implement or tool, especially a prong of a fork or a tooth of a comb.
imi: symbol. ISO 639-3 language code for Anamgura.
immit: verb. To send in, put in, insert, inject or infuse
mei: symbol. ISO 639-3 language code for Midob.
ment: verb. simple past and past participle of meng
neti: noun. Nasal irrigation performed using a neti pot as part of Hatha yoga.
nne: symbol. ISO 639-3 language code for Ngandyera.
tem: symbol. ISO 639-2 & ISO 639-3 language code for Temne.
tim: symbol. ISO 639-3 language code for Timbe.
